---
title: "Greenland Glacier Retreat Susceptibility Application"
format:
  html:
    css: tools/style.css
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-location: right
    page-layout: article
    title-block-banner: images/Greenland_title.png
    code-overflow: scroll
---

```{=html}
<a class="github-top-button" href="https://github.com/Entao21/bd_group" target="_blank" rel="noopener">GitHub Repo</a>
```

## Project Summary

Greenland's ice sheet is losing mass rapidly, with an average loss of about 150 ± 13 Gt per year between 1992 and 2020 ([IMBIE Team, 2020](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-019-1855-2)). That amout of ice loss resulted in Greenland ice loss alone contributed to 14.7% of global sea-level rise per year.([IMBIE Team, 2020](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-019-1855-2); [Otosaka et al., 2023](https://essd.copernicus.org/articles/15/1597/2023/); [van den Broeke et al., 2016](https://tc.copernicus.org/articles/10/1933/2016/)). Climate change, global warming, human activities,  mineral exploration, and many other factors are all threatening this sensitive cryospheric environment ([Meredith et al.,2024](https://www.ipcc.ch/srocc/chapter/chapter-3-2/)). 

This project develops an interactive GEE application to explore glacier retreat susceptibility across Greenland's peripheral glacier zone. This app measures the combined effect of physical and human factors on peripheral glacier retreat. A Random Forest model is trained on observed retreat and stable-ice labels to map where current glaciers are more susceptible to retreat.

### Problem Statement

Among all parts of Greenland's ice sheet, peripheral glaciers are the most vulnerable ones. According to [Larocca et al., 2023](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41558-023-01855-6), peripheral glaciers are not only melting at a faster rate than the central ice sheet, but their melting speed is also accelerating more rapidly than the central. Research by [Khan et al., 2022](https://doi.org/10.1029/2022GL098915)found that 4% of the peripheral ice sheet contributed about 14% of total Greenland ice loss ([Khan et al., 2022](https://doi.org/10.1029/2022GL098915)).

Existing tools exhibits huge gap of showing peripheral glacier retreat susceptibility. Our application is aiming for combining change detection, climate trends, topographic controls and human intervention indicators into a single exploratory geospatial tool that helps users investigate where these factors align with that gap by increased glacier retreat susceptibility. The easy-to-use interface greatly improve the size of the target users.

### End User

This application is designed for users who need a clear spatial overview of the glacier retreat susceptibility of Greenland's peripheral glaciers. Potential users include:

- Arctic environmental researchers
- Greenland planning and resource governance agencies
- Environmental NGOs
- Local residents
- Students

The interface supports these users by combining technical model outputs with accessible interaction. Users can compare historical and recent glacier extent, toggle model layers, inspect model diagnostics, and click anywhere on the map to retrieve local glacier, temperature, elevation, and susceptibility statistics.

Github repository and source code: [https://github.com/Entao21/bd_group](https://github.com/Entao21/bd_group). The application is built on GEE platform.  


### Data

<style>
  .custom-data-table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
    font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif;
    color: #2c3e50;
    line-height: 1.6;
    background-color: #ffffff;
    border: 1px solid #d4e3f1;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
    font-size: 1.05rem; 
  }

  .custom-data-table th {
    background-color: #f0f7fd !important;
    color: #1a4a75;
    text-align: left;
    padding: 14px 18px;
    font-weight: 700;
    border-bottom: 2px solid #3498db;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  }

  .custom-data-table td {
    padding: 16px 18px;
    vertical-align: top;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #eef4f9;
  }

  .custom-data-table tr:hover td {
    background-color: #f0f7fd !important;
    transition: background-color 0.2s ease;
  }

  .custom-data-table td[rowspan] {
    background-color: #ffffff;
    font-weight: 700;
    color: #1a4a75;
    vertical-align: middle;
    border-right: 1px solid #eef4f9;
    text-align: center;
  }

  .custom-data-table a {
    color: #3498db;
    text-decoration: none;
    font-weight: 600;
  }

  .custom-data-table a:hover {
    text-decoration: underline;
  }

  .dataset-name {
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #1a4a75;
    display: block;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
  }

  .desc-text {
    font-size: 0.95rem; 
    color: #546e7a;
    display: block;
    margin-top: 6px;
  }
</style>



<table class="custom-data-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Category</th>
      <th>Dataset</th>
      <th>Description</th>
      <th>Source</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td rowspan="4">GEE</td>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Terrain (ArcticDEM)</span></td>
      <td>UMN/PGC ArcticDEM V4.1 2m mosaic. Resampled using <b>bilinear interpolation</b> to <b>300m scale</b> to serve as elevation and slope predictors for Greenland-wide susceptibility modelling.</td>
      <td><a href="https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/UMN_PGC_ArcticDEM_V4_2m_mosaic" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Satellite Imagery (Landsat)</span></td>
      <td>Landsat 7/8/9 Collection 2 Level-2. Multi-temporal summer composites used to map glacier extent via <b>NDSI thresholding (>0.4)</b> and determine <b>Land Surface Temperature (LST)</b> using ST bands.</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.usgs.gov/landsat-missions/landsat-collection-2" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Ice Mask (OSU GIMP)</span></td>
      <td>OSU GIMP 2000 Ice/Ocean Mask. Used to delineate the <b>peripheral glacier zone</b> (ice < 2500m elevation or south of 64°N) to isolate analytical focus from the interior ice sheet.</td>
      <td><a href="https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/OSU_GIMP_2000_ICE_OCEAN_MASK" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Boundaries (LSIB)</span></td>
      <td>USDOS Large Scale International Boundary (LSIB) Simplified 2017. Defines the official <b>Greenland spatial extent</b> and study area geometry (AOI) for all subsequent image clipping and analysis.</td>
      <td><a href="https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/USDOS_LSIB_SIMPLE_2017" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td rowspan="4">Other</td>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Mining Activity</span></td>
      <td>Active industrial mineral licences and drillhole points (post-2000). Processed into <b>proximity predictors</b> using <code>cumulativeCost</code> analysis (maxDist: 100km) to quantify anthropogenic pressure.</td>
      <td><a href="https://data.geus.dk/geusmap/?mapname=greenland_portal&lang=en#baslay=&optlay=&extent=-2877925.4866235047,6437557.7337947935,2824736.5504135317,9535590.1412022&layers=g250_topographic_map_utm24n" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Settlements & Cities</span></td>
      <td>Official settlement and city point layers from Asiaq. Used to generate <b>accessibility indicators</b> via cost-distance analysis, representing the proximity of glaciers to human infrastructure.</td>
      <td><a href="https://kortvisning.asiaq.gl/" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Reference Grid</span></td>
      <td>15-degree graticules (Natural Earth). Provides <b>polar map orientation</b> and coordinate reference within the interactive application to maintain geographic context at varying zoom levels.</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/10m-physical-vectors/10m-graticules/" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span class="dataset-name">Admin Boundaries</span></td>
      <td>GADM Greenland (g1bd) boundaries. Integrated to provide <b>regional context</b> for click-based statistics and to help users identify administrative districts during vulnerability assessments.</td>
      <td><a href="https://gadm.org/download_country.html" target="_blank">Link</a></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### Methodology

The pipeline runs in three steps. Preprocessing, predictor modelling, and machine learning. The figure below is an overview of this pipeline. Raw inputs produce a seven-variable predictor stack, and then a random forest model is trained on observed retreat and stable-ice labels and exports the results as an GEE image asset. 

The interactive app then loads both assets alongside uploaded vector layers to build the split-panel interface so there is no heavy computation at runtime.

```{=html}
<div style="background:#f5f5f5;padding:24px 20px;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid #e0e0e0;font-family:Arial,sans-serif;font-size:13px;margin:16px 0;text-align:center;">

  <div style="font-size:11px;color:#888;font-style:italic;margin-bottom:12px;text-align:left;">Step 1 &nbsp;·&nbsp; gee_preprocess_export.js — preprocessing &amp; model training</div>

  <div style="display:inline-flex;gap:10px;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:6px;">
    <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">ArcticDEM</div>
    <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Landsat 7/8/9</div>
    <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Vector assets</div>
    <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">AOI boundary</div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:inline-flex;gap:10px;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:6px;color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;">
    <div style="min-width:110px;">↓</div>
    <div style="min-width:110px;">↓</div>
    <div style="min-width:110px;">↓</div>
    <div style="min-width:110px;">↓</div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:inline-flex;gap:10px;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:10px;">
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:10px 12px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Terrain<br><span style="font-size:11px;opacity:0.85;">Elev + slope</span></div>
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:10px 12px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Glacier NDSI<br><span style="font-size:11px;opacity:0.85;">2000 vs 2024</span></div>
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:10px 12px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">LST trend<br><span style="font-size:11px;opacity:0.85;">Warming rate</span></div>
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:10px 12px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Proximity<br><span style="font-size:11px;opacity:0.85;">3 distance rasters</span></div>
  </div>

  <div style="color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;margin-bottom:8px;">↓</div>

  <div style="display:inline-block;background:#4527a0;color:#fff;padding:12px 40px;border-radius:6px;min-width:320px;margin-bottom:8px;">
    Random Forest (100 trees)<br><span style="font-size:11px;opacity:0.85;">7 predictors · spatial block CV</span>
  </div>

  <div style="color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;margin-bottom:8px;">↓</div>

  <div style="display:inline-block;background:#795548;color:#fff;padding:12px 40px;border-radius:6px;min-width:320px;margin-bottom:14px;">
    8-band image asset<br><span style="font-size:11px;color:#ffcc80;">Exported to GEE asset</span>
  </div>

  <div style="color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;margin-bottom:8px;">↓</div>

  <div style="border:1.5px dashed #bbb;border-radius:6px;padding:14px 20px;margin:0 auto 10px auto;display:inline-block;min-width:500px;">
    <div style="font-size:11px;color:#888;font-style:italic;margin-bottom:10px;text-align:left;">Step 2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; gee_context_export.js — batch export</div>
    <div style="display:inline-flex;gap:20px;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:8px;">
      <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 18px;border-radius:5px;">Precomputed elev</div>
      <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 18px;border-radius:5px;">GIMP ice mask</div>
    </div>
    <div style="color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;margin-bottom:8px;">↓</div>
    <div style="display:inline-block;background:#795548;color:#fff;padding:12px 40px;border-radius:6px;min-width:280px;">
      4-band context asset
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;margin-bottom:8px;">↓</div>

  <div style="border:1.5px dashed #bbb;border-radius:6px;padding:14px 20px;margin:0 auto;display:inline-block;min-width:500px;">
    <div style="font-size:11px;color:#888;font-style:italic;margin-bottom:10px;text-align:left;">Step 3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; gee_glacier_mining_app.js — interactive app</div>
    <div style="display:inline-flex;gap:12px;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:8px;">
      <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Precomputed asset<br><span style="font-size:10px;opacity:0.8;">from Step 1</span></div>
      <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Context asset<br><span style="font-size:10px;opacity:0.8;">from Step 2</span></div>
      <div style="background:#455a64;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;min-width:110px;">Vector layers<br><span style="font-size:10px;opacity:0.8;">GEE uploads</span></div>
    </div>
    <div style="color:#90a4ae;font-size:16px;margin-bottom:8px;">↓</div>
    <div style="display:inline-block;background:#7f1d1d;color:#fff;padding:12px 40px;border-radius:6px;min-width:280px;">
      Interactive split panel<br><span style="font-size:11px;color:#ffb3b3;">Swipe · click · analyse</span>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

The sections below describe each processing step in detail.

#### Glacier Change Detection and Preprocessing

```{=html}
<div style="background:#f5f5f5;padding:22px 24px;border-radius:8px;font-family:Arial,sans-serif;font-size:13px;margin:14px 0;overflow-x:auto;border:1px solid #e0e0e0;">
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;min-width:700px;">
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:7px;">
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:120px;">Landsat 7<br>L2 Scenes</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:120px;">Landsat 8<br>L2 Scenes</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:120px;">Landsat 9<br>L2 Scenes</div>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#546e7a;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:128px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">Cloud Mask<br>(QA_PIXEL)</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#0277bd;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:128px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">NDSI<br>Calculation</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#01579b;color:#fff;width:46px;height:46px;border-radius:50%;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:12px;text-align:center;font-weight:bold;">&gt; 0.4</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:7px;">
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:110px;">Glacier Mask<br>~2000</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:110px;">Glacier Mask<br>~2024</div>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#546e7a;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:128px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">Patch<br>Filter<br>(≥ 3 px)</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#01579b;color:#fff;padding:14px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:100px;">Retreat<br>Extent<br>(Δ ice)</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

Glacier extent for two time windows ~2000 (1999–2002) and ~2024 (2022–2024) was mapped from Landsat summer composites (June–August). Each scene was cloud-masked using the QA_PIXEL band, and the Normalized Difference Snow Index (NDSI) was calculated from the green and SWIR bands (Bands 2 & 5 for Landsat 7; Bands 3 & 6 for Landsat 8/9). Pixels with NDSI > 0.4 were classified as glacier ice. Retreat is defined as any pixel that was ice in ~2000 but not in ~2024. Small isolated patches (fewer than 3 connected pixels) were removed from both retreat and stable-ice labels to reduce noise from cloud shadows and Landsat 7 scan-line gaps. All output rasters were standardized to a 300 m polar grid (EPSG:3413).

```js
// Cloud masking using QA_PIXEL for Landsat 8/9
function maskL8(image) {
  var qa = image.select('QA_PIXEL');
  return image.updateMask(
    qa.bitwiseAnd(1 << 3).eq(0)          // cloud
      .and(qa.bitwiseAnd(1 << 4).eq(0))  // cloud shadow
  );
}

// NDSI from green and SWIR bands (Landsat 8/9)
function ndsiL8(img) {
  return img.addBands(
    img.normalizedDifference(['SR_B3', 'SR_B6']).rename('NDSI'));
}

// Build median NDSI composite and threshold at 0.4
function getGlacierExtent(startYear, endYear) {
  var t0 = ee.Date.fromYMD(startYear, 1, 1);
  var t1 = ee.Date.fromYMD(endYear, 12, 31);
  var summer = ee.Filter.calendarRange(6, 8, 'month');

  var l8 = ee.ImageCollection('LANDSAT/LC08/C02/T1_L2')
    .filterBounds(greenlandBox).filterDate(t0, t1)
    .filter(summer).map(maskL8).map(ndsiL8);
  var l9 = ee.ImageCollection('LANDSAT/LC09/C02/T1_L2')
    .filterBounds(greenlandBox).filterDate(t0, t1)
    .filter(summer).map(maskL8).map(ndsiL8);

  return l8.merge(l9).select('NDSI').median()
    .gt(0.4).rename('ice').clip(AOI_geom).unmask(0);
}

var glacier_2000 = getGlacierExtent(1999, 2002).rename('glacier_2000');
var glacier_2024 = getGlacierExtent(2022, 2024).rename('glacier_2024');

// Retreat = ice in 2000 but not in 2024
var retreatCandidate = glacier_2000.subtract(glacier_2024).max(0).gt(0);
var stableCandidate  = glacier_2000.eq(1).and(glacier_2024.eq(1));

// Remove isolated patches smaller than minPatchPixels (set to 3)
var cleanRetreat = retreatCandidate.selfMask()
  .connectedPixelCount(8, true).gte(minPatchPixels)
  .unmask(0).toByte().rename('retreat');

var cleanStable = stableCandidate.selfMask()
  .connectedPixelCount(8, true).gte(minPatchPixels)
  .unmask(0).toByte().rename('stable');
```

#### Climate Trend and Predictor Modelling

```{=html}
<div style="background:#f5f5f5;padding:22px 24px;border-radius:8px;font-family:Arial,sans-serif;font-size:13px;margin:14px 0;overflow-x:auto;border:1px solid #e0e0e0;">
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;min-width:680px;">
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:7px;">
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:120px;">Landsat 7/8/9<br>ST Bands</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:120px;">2001–2024<br>Summers</div>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#546e7a;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:100px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">K → °C<br>Calibration</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#0277bd;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:100px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">Annual<br>Compositing</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:14px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:100px;">24 Annual<br>LST Means</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#546e7a;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:100px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">Per-pixel<br>Linear<br>Fit (OLS)</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#01579b;color:#fff;padding:14px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:100px;">Warming Rate<br>(°C / yr)</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

To track how temperatures have changed, Landsat surface temperature (ST) scenes from 2000 to 2024 were converted from raw DN values to degrees Celsius, then averaged into one composite per summer (June–August). Fitting a straight line through all 24 annual means gives a warming rate (°C/year) for every pixel. Two snapshot composites, one for ~2000 and one for ~2024 were also retained so users can compare absolute temperatures directly in the app. Elevation and slope were derived from ArcticDEM v4 at the same 300 m resolution. Together with three proximity rasters, these variables make up the full predictor set:

| Predictor | Source |
|-----------|--------|
| Elevation | ArcticDEM v4 |
| Slope | Derived from ArcticDEM |
| Warming rate | Landsat ST linear trend 2001–2024 |
| Mean summer LST | Landsat ST ~2000 composite |
| Mine proximity | Drillholes + licence centroids (0–100 km) |
| Settlement proximity | Settlements + cities (0–100 km) |
| Coast proximity | Greenland boundary (0–300 km) |

```js
// Convert Landsat ST_B10 DN to Celsius
function lstL8(image) {
  return image.select('ST_B10')
    .multiply(0.00341802).add(149.0).subtract(273.15)
    .rename('LST_C')
    .copyProperties(image, ['system:time_start']);
}

// Build annual summer LST composites for 2001-2024
var years = ee.List.sequence(2001, 2024);

var annualLST = ee.ImageCollection(years.map(function(year) {
  year = ee.Number(year);
  var s = ee.Date.fromYMD(year, 6, 1);
  var e = ee.Date.fromYMD(year, 8, 31);
  var l8 = ee.ImageCollection('LANDSAT/LC08/C02/T1_L2')
    .filterBounds(greenlandBox).filterDate(s, e).map(maskL8).map(lstL8);
  var l9 = ee.ImageCollection('LANDSAT/LC09/C02/T1_L2')
    .filterBounds(greenlandBox).filterDate(s, e).map(maskL8).map(lstL8);
  return l8.merge(l9).mean()
    .set('year', year).set('system:time_start', s.millis());
}));

// Attach numeric time band then run OLS — slope = °C/yr
var withTime = annualLST.map(function(img) {
  return img.addBands(
    ee.Image.constant(ee.Number(img.get('year'))).float().rename('time'));
});

var warmingRate = withTime.select(['time', 'LST_C'])
  .reduce(ee.Reducer.linearFit())
  .select('scale').clip(AOI_geom).rename('warming_rate');

// Proximity rasters: inverse cumulative cost distance, scaled 0-1
function distanceToFC(fc, maxDist) {
  var source = ee.Image(0).byte().paint(
    fc.map(function(f) { return f.buffer(1000); }), 1);
  return ee.Image(1).cumulativeCost(source, maxDist).unmask(maxDist);
}

var mineProximity = ee.Image(1)
  .subtract(distanceToFC(allMines, 100000).divide(100000))
  .max(0).min(1).rename('mine_proximity');

var settlementProximity = ee.Image(1)
  .subtract(distanceToFC(allSettlements, 100000).divide(100000))
  .max(0).min(1).rename('settlement_proximity');

var coastProximity = ee.Image(1)
  .subtract(distToCoast.divide(300000))
  .max(0).min(1).rename('coast_proximity');
```

#### Random Forest Retreat Susceptibility Model

```{=html}
<div style="background:#f5f5f5;padding:22px 24px 18px;border-radius:8px;font-family:Arial,sans-serif;font-size:13px;margin:14px 0;overflow-x:auto;border:1px solid #e0e0e0;">
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;margin-bottom:6px;padding-left:200px;">
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Retreat<br>Labels</div>
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Stable Ice<br>Labels</div>
    <div style="font-size:20px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#0277bd;color:#fff;padding:7px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Spatial Block Cross-Validation<br>(folds 0–6 train · 7–9 test)</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding-left:480px;margin-bottom:4px;font-size:20px;color:#90a4ae;">↓</div>
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;min-width:700px;">
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;">
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:130px;">Elevation</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Slope</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Warming Rate</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Mean Summer LST</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Mine Proximity</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Settlement Proximity</div>
      <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:7px 12px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;">Coast Proximity</div>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#546e7a;color:#fff;padding:9px 8px;border-radius:5px;writing-mode:vertical-lr;transform:rotate(180deg);height:238px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;text-align:center;">Mask to<br>Peripheral<br>Zone</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#01579b;color:#fff;padding:38px 16px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:160px;">Random Forest<br>(100 Trees)<br><br>setOutputMode<br>('REGRESSION')</div>
    <div style="font-size:22px;color:#90a4ae;">→</div>
    <div style="background:#1565c0;color:#fff;padding:22px 14px;border-radius:5px;text-align:center;min-width:100px;">Susceptibility<br>Score<br>(0–1)</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

A Random Forest with 100 trees was then trained to score how likely each current glacier pixel is to retreat. Only pixels with clear historical retreat or clear stable-ice history were used as training examples and ambiguous pixels were left out. The model only runs on peripheral glaciers (GIMP land ice below 2500 m, plus all ice south of 64°N where the southern ice cap behaves like a peripheral glacier rather than the interior ice sheet).

To avoid the problem where nearby pixels leak from training into the test set, a spatial block validation split the map into 10 geographic chunks. Chunks 0–6 trained the model and chunks 7–9 tested it. 

A second, classification-mode version of the same model produced the accuracy, kappa, precision, recall, and F1 scores shown in the app panel. The final score (0–1) is the fraction of the 100 trees that voted for retreat, assigned to every glacier pixel visible in 2022–2024.

```js
// Build spatial folds from a lon/lat grid (10 folds total)
var lonLat = ee.Image.pixelLonLat();
var spatialFold = lonLat.select('longitude').add(180).divide(2).floor()
  .add(lonLat.select('latitude').add(90).divide(2).floor().multiply(13))
  .mod(10).rename('spatial_fold');

// Only sample clean retreat / stable pixels inside the model domain
var sampleMask = cleanRetreat.eq(1).or(cleanStable.eq(1)).and(peripheralMask);

var samples = predictors.addBands(target).addBands(spatialFold)
  .updateMask(sampleMask)
  .stratifiedSample({
    numPoints: 2000, classBand: 'retreat',
    region: greenlandBox, scale: exportScale, seed: 42,
    geometries: false, tileScale: 4
  });

var trainSet = samples.filter(ee.Filter.lt('spatial_fold', 7));
var testSet  = samples.filter(ee.Filter.gte('spatial_fold', 7));

// Classification RF → confusion matrix and performance stats
var rfClassify = ee.Classifier.smileRandomForest({numberOfTrees: 100, seed: 42})
  .train({ features: trainSet, classProperty: 'retreat',
           inputProperties: bandNames });

// Regression RF → continuous susceptibility score
var rfPredict = ee.Classifier.smileRandomForest({numberOfTrees: 100, seed: 42})
  .setOutputMode('REGRESSION')
  .train({ features: trainSet, classProperty: 'retreat',
           inputProperties: bandNames });

// Evaluate on held-out spatial blocks
var confusionMatrix = testSet.classify(rfClassify)
  .errorMatrix('retreat', 'classification', [0, 1]);
var precision = tp.divide(tp.add(fp).max(1));
var recall    = tp.divide(tp.add(fn).max(1));
var f1 = precision.multiply(recall).multiply(2)
           .divide(precision.add(recall).max(0.001));

// Score only current glacier pixels
var susceptibilityMap = predictors
  .updateMask(glacier_2024.eq(1).and(peripheralMask))
  .classify(rfPredict)
  .rename('susceptibility').max(0).min(1);
```

#### Precomputed Asset Architecture

Running Landsat composites, distance rasters, and the Random Forest across all of Greenland takes too long to repeat every time someone opens the app. For our application, everything is computed once and saved as a single multi-band Earth Engine image asset that the app reads at startup. Model performance metrics are embedded as asset metadata so the app can display them without re-running any calculations.

| Band | Content |
|------|---------|
| `glacier_2000` | Binary glacier mask ~2000 |
| `glacier_2024` | Binary glacier mask ~2024 |
| `retreat` | Cleaned retreat pixels |
| `warming_rate` | LST trend (°C/yr, 2001–2024) |
| `lst_early` | Mean summer LST ~2000 |
| `lst_recent` | Mean summer LST ~2024 |
| `susceptibility` | RF retreat susceptibility (0–1) |
| `elevation` | ArcticDEM elevation (m) |

### Interface

The interface uses a linked split map. The left map shows the approximate 2000 glacier extent and early-period temperature context. The right map shows the approximate 2024 glacier extent, observed retreat, warming rate, susceptibility prediction, mining sites, and settlements. A swipe divider allows direct visual comparison between baseline and recent glacier conditions.

After clicking 'future risk of melting', the interface will close the 2000 map and only show the future risk of retreat.

The side panel provides:

- two function buttons: '2000 vs 2024 glacier content' and 'future risk of melting'
- model performance statistics from the exported asset;
- a variable-importance chart;
- susceptibility and domain legends;
- a click-based statistics panel.

When users click the map, the app summarises a 25 km radius region around the click location. It reports glacier area change, mean temperature, warming trend, elevation, domain status, and mean retreat susceptibility.

```js
var region = point.buffer(25000);

var stats = ee.Dictionary({
  area2000: glacier_2000.multiply(ee.Image.pixelArea()).reduceRegion({
    reducer: ee.Reducer.sum(),
    geometry: region,
    scale: clickStatsScale,
    maxPixels: 1e9
  }).get('glacier_2000'),

  susceptibility: susceptibilityMap.reduceRegion({
    reducer: ee.Reducer.mean(),
    geometry: region,
    scale: clickStatsScale,
    maxPixels: 1e9
  }).get('susceptibility')
});
```

## The Application

### Application Overview

Powered by Google Earth Engine, this application investigates the intersection of glacier retreat, climate warming, and industrial expansion across Greenland. It enables users to synchronize temporal views (2000 vs. 2024), analyze warming trends, and assess the proximity of mining licenses to sensitive glacial zones. By integrating a Random Forest susceptibility model, the tool provides real-time insights to support environmental monitoring and spatial planning in rapidly changing Arctic landscapes.

:::{.column-screen}

<iframe src='https://ee-k24081637.projects.earthengine.app/view/greenland-glacier-mining' width='100%' height='90vh' style='height:90vh; border:none;'></iframe>

:::

### Key Features & Backend Optimization

While the interface is designed for simplicity, several critical features work behind the scenes to optimize performance and ensure a seamless, data-driven user experience.

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
#| classes: '.g-col-lg-12 .g-col-12'
source("tools/ppt.R")
carousel("gallery-carousel", 5000, 
         yaml.load_file("tools/ppt.yml"))
```

## How it Works

### 1. Data Preparation and Precomputations

The app is organised as a three-script workflow. The first script, `gee_preprocess_export.js`, creates the remote-sensing and machine-learning layers described in the Methodology section. The second script, `gee_context_export.js`, exports lightweight context masks that separate the modelled peripheral glacier zone from the central/northern ice-sheet context. The final script, `gee_glacier_mining_app.js`, loads these exported assets and builds the user interface.

This separation is important because Greenland-wide Landsat compositing, distance rasters, and Random Forest inference are too computationally expensive to run whenever a user opens the app. Instead, the app reads a precomputed image asset:

```js
var precomputed = ee.Image(
  'projects/ee-k24081637/assets/0025/greenland_glacier_mining_precomputed'
);

var contextPrecomputed = ee.Image(
  'projects/ee-k24081637/assets/0025/greenland_display_context_precomputed'
);

var glacier_2000 = precomputed.select('glacier_2000');
var glacier_2024 = precomputed.select('glacier_2024');
var retreatOnly  = precomputed.select('retreat').selfMask();
var warmingRate  = precomputed.select('warming_rate');
var susceptibilityMap = precomputed.select('susceptibility');
```

The processing steps behind these assets including NDSI glacier mapping, LST trend fitting, proximity rasters, and Random Forest training are described in full in the Methodology section above. Model performance metrics (accuracy, kappa, precision, recall, F1) are stored as asset metadata and displayed in the app side panel without re-running any calculations.

### 2. Interactive Visualization and Analysis

The main interface is a linked split map. The left map presents baseline glacier conditions around 2000, while the right map presents recent glacier extent, observed retreat, warming, mining/settlement overlays, and the Random Forest retreat-susceptibility prediction.

```js
var leftMap  = ui.Map();
var rightMap = ui.Map();

leftMap.setOptions('SATELLITE');
rightMap.setOptions('SATELLITE');
leftMap.setCenter(-42, 72, 4);

var linker = ui.Map.Linker([leftMap, rightMap]);

leftMap.addLayer(glacier_2000.selfMask(), glacierVis2000,
  'Glacier Extent ~2000', true);

rightMap.addLayer(glacier_2024.selfMask(), glacierVis2024,
  'Glacier Extent ~2024', true);
rightMap.addLayer(retreatOnly, retreatVis,
  'Glacier Retreat (ice lost)', false);
rightMap.addLayer(susceptibilityMap, susceptibilityVis,
  'Retreat Susceptibility Prediction', true);
```

The draggable split panel allows users to compare historical and recent conditions directly:

```js
var splitPanel = ui.SplitPanel({
  firstPanel: leftMap,
  secondPanel: rightMap,
  wipe: true,
  style: {stretch: 'both'}
});

ui.root.clear();
ui.root.add(splitPanel);
```

The side panel displays model diagnostics from asset metadata rather than recalculating them in the app session:

```js
ee.Dictionary({
  accuracy: precomputed.get('accuracy'),
  kappa: precomputed.get('kappa'),
  precision: precomputed.get('precision'),
  recall: precomputed.get('recall'),
  f1: precomputed.get('f1'),
  nTrain: precomputed.get('n_train'),
  nTest: precomputed.get('n_test')
}).evaluate(function(st) {
  modelStatsPanel.clear();
  modelStatsPanel.add(ui.Label(
    'Accuracy: ' + (st.accuracy * 100).toFixed(1) + '%'
  ));
  modelStatsPanel.add(ui.Label(
    'F1 score: ' + st.f1.toFixed(3)
  ));
});
```

### 3. Click-Based Regional Statistics

To make the map actionable, users can click any location to generate statistics within a 25 km buffer. The app reports glacier area change, mean temperature, warming trend, elevation, domain status and mean susceptibility.

```js
function handleMapClick(coords) {
  var point  = ee.Geometry.Point([coords.lon, coords.lat]);
  var region = point.buffer(25000);
  var pxArea = ee.Image.pixelArea();

  var stats = ee.Dictionary({
    area2000: glacier_2000.multiply(pxArea).reduceRegion({
      reducer: ee.Reducer.sum(),
      geometry: region,
      scale: clickStatsScale,
      maxPixels: 1e9
    }).get('glacier_2000'),

    area2024: glacier_2024.multiply(pxArea).reduceRegion({
      reducer: ee.Reducer.sum(),
      geometry: region,
      scale: clickStatsScale,
      maxPixels: 1e9
    }).get('glacier_2024'),

    susceptibility: susceptibilityMap.reduceRegion({
      reducer: ee.Reducer.mean(),
      geometry: region,
      scale: clickStatsScale,
      maxPixels: 1e9
    }).get('susceptibility')
  });
}
```

The app also checks whether the selected location falls inside the modelled peripheral glacier zone or only in the ice-sheet context layer. This prevents users from over-interpreting susceptibility outside the intended model domain.

```js
var inModelDomain = s.domain !== null && s.domain > 0;
var inContextOnly = !inModelDomain && s.context !== null && s.context > 0;

var domainText = inModelDomain ? 'Modelled peripheral glacier zone' :
                 inContextOnly ? 'Ice-sheet context only' :
                 'Outside model domain';
```

### 4. Limitations and Potential Expansion

The main limitations are data-related. Landsat NDSI may confuse seasonal snow, clouds, shadows and glacier ice; Landsat 7 baseline imagery may contain SLC-off gaps; and very high northern Greenland has weaker routine Landsat coverage. ArcticDEM is a mosaic rather than a single-date surface, so elevation and slope do not exactly match every Landsat year. GIMP is a static 2000 ice/ocean mask, while mining licences and drillholes indicate legal or exploration activity rather than confirmed active production. Settlement points are simplified accessibility indicators, and the 300 m modelling scale may smooth narrow outlet glaciers.

The susceptibility model therefore identifies spatial association, not causation. Mining proximity should be interpreted as an exploratory pressure indicator. Future work could compare a natural-only model with a full model including mining and settlement proximity:

```js
var naturalBands = [
  'elevation', 'slope', 'warming_rate', 'lst_mean', 'coast_proximity'
];

var fullBands = [
  'elevation', 'slope', 'warming_rate', 'lst_mean',
  'coast_proximity', 'mine_proximity', 'settlement_proximity'
];
```

## References

- IMBIE Team (2020) 'Mass balance of the Greenland Ice Sheet from 1992 to 2018', *Nature*, 579, pp. 233-239. Available at: <https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-019-1855-2> (Accessed: 27 April 2026).
- Otosaka, I.N., Shepherd, A., Ivins, E.R. *et al.* (2023) 'Mass balance of the Greenland and Antarctic ice sheets from 1992 to 2020', *Earth System Science Data*, 15(4), pp. 1597-1616. Available at: <https://essd.copernicus.org/articles/15/1597/2023/> (Accessed: 27 April 2026).
- van den Broeke, M., Enderlin, E., Howat, I. *et al.* (2016) 'On the recent contribution of the Greenland ice sheet to sea level change', *The Cryosphere*, 10, pp. 1933-1946. Available at: <https://tc.copernicus.org/articles/10/1933/2016/> (Accessed: 27 April 2026).
- Khan, S.A., Colgan, W., Neumann, T.A. *et al.* (2022) 'Accelerating ice loss from peripheral glaciers in North Greenland', *Geophysical Research Letters*, 49(12), e2022GL098915. Available at: <https://doi.org/10.1029/2022GL098915> (Accessed: 27 April 2026).
- Larocca, L.J., Twining-Ward, M., Axford, Y. *et al.* (2023) 'Greenland-wide accelerated retreat of peripheral glaciers in the twenty-first century', *Nature Climate Change*, 13, pp. 1324-1328. Available at: <https://www.nature.com/articles/s41558-023-01855-6> (Accessed: 27 April 2026).
